title: Sunwoo Kwak
text:
I am currently a Master’s student in Political Science at Yonsei University (Seoul, South Korea).
I will be joining Princeton University as a Ph.D. student in the Department of Politics starting in August 2026.
My research interests include:

American Politics
Legislative Studies and Congress
Political Parties and Elections
Political Communication and Public Opinion

So far, my research has been published in Legislative Studies Quarterly and in Korean-language journals.

title: Research
text:
Publications

Kwak, Sunwoo, Eunmi Cho, and Sinjae Kang. “Who Controls the Legislative Process? Party Cartels, Committee Gatekeepers, and Subcommittee Activity in the South Korean National Assembly.” Legislative Studies Quarterly, Published Online.

Link

Kang, Sinjae, Sunwoo Kwak, and Jeongmin Lee. 2025. “Did the 2019 Revision of the National Assembly Act Revitalize Subcommittees? Analyzing the Frequency and Scheduling Determinants of Bill Review Subcommittee Meetings.” Legislation and Policy Studies, 17(1): 5–36. [in Korean]

Link

Terashita, Kazuhiro, Eunmi Cho, Juhyeon Jeong, and Sunwoo Kwak. 2024. “Who Mentions the Region?: Analyzing Geographical Representation through Parliamentary Speeches.” Journal of Parliamentary Research, 19(2): 5–42. [in Korean]

title: Research
